channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: “Lula está do lado dos criminosos”, diz Moro em evento do PL
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro formally joined the PL party during an event in Curitiba alongside Flávio Bolsonaro, the party's pre-candidate for president. At the event, Moro questioned the legitimacy of Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's election and accused the federal government of being on the side of criminals, criticizing its public security policy.

item.title: Governo Lula e estados negociam subsídio extra de R$ 1,20 por litro de diesel importado
item.description: The Lula government is negotiating with states an additional subsidy of R$ 1,20 per liter of imported diesel, to be split equally between the federal government and states, as an alternative to reducing ICMS. The measure, to be discussed at Confaz on March 27, would add to existing benefits (PIS/Cofins zero and R$ 0.32/liter subsidy), totaling R$ 1.84/liter in fuel price relief. The estimated impact is R$ 3 billion over two months.

item.title: Moraes concede prisão domiciliar a Bolsonaro após parecer da PGR e apelo de Michelle
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted humanitarian house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro, who is hospitalized in Brasília due to health complications. The decision followed a favorable opinion from the Prosecutor General's Office and an appeal by former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro. Additional highlights include the trial that could render former Rio governor Cláudio Castro ineligible and the expected extension of the INSS CPMI, to be reviewed by the Court.

item.title: Tornozeleira, visitas restritas e sem celular e redes; veja regras da domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted house arrest to former President Jair Bolsonaro for humanitarian reasons, but imposed strict conditions: electronic ankle monitor, ban on cell phones and social media, and restricted visits limited to family and lawyers. The ruling aims to uphold public order while addressing Bolsonaro’s health needs.

item.title: Justiça mantém condenação de criminalista que chamou Moraes de ‘advogado do PCC’
item.description: São Paulo's 3rd Private Law Chamber upheld the defamation conviction of criminal lawyer Celso Machado Vendramini for calling Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes a 'PCC lawyer'. The statements, made during a 2023 jury session, were ruled as exceeding legitimate criticism and carrying personal, ideological, and defamatory intent. The court affirmed the R$ 50,000 moral damages award, dismissing Vendramini's claim of professional immunity.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro chama prisão domiciliar temporária de ‘exótica’ e Carlos vê ‘migalha ditatorial’
item.description: Flávio and Carlos Bolsonaro criticized the STF's decision to grant temporary house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro, calling it 'exotic' and a 'dictatorial crumb,' respectively, despite expressing relief that their father can recover at home. Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos-SP) welcomed the move, emphasizing the importance of humane, family-centered care for the former president's recovery.

item.title: Lula sanciona Lei Antifacção e ressalta prisão de magnatas do crime
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the Anti-Gang Law, which increases penalties for participation in criminal organizations, facilitates asset seizure, and removes benefits such as amnesty and voting rights for gang leaders, even without final conviction. Lula emphasized that the law targets 'crime magnates' operating from luxury apartments, not just poor youth, and praised the Federal Police’s investigative expertise. He also noted discussions with U.S. President Donald Trump on transnational crime cooperation.

item.title: Governo arrecadará R$ 4,4 bi com tributos de fintechs, bets e JCP
item.description: The Lula government projects to collect R$ 4.4 billion in 2026 from taxes on fintechs, sports betting (bets), and interest on own capital (JCP), as announced by Federal Revenue Secretary Robinson Barreirinhas. Measures include raising the CSLL for financial institutions and increasing the JCP rate from 15% to 17.5%, plus taxation of GGR from bets. Despite this, the government estimates a primary surplus of only R$ 3.5 billion in 2026, far below the budget target of R$ 34.3 billion, due to tax benefit cuts and freezing of discretionary spending.

item.title: Lula sanciona PL Antifacção com apenas 2 vetos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the Anti-Gang Bill with only two vetoes: one to prevent the criminalization of individuals not linked to criminal organizations and another regarding the unconstitutional allocation of Funad funds. National Public Security Secretary Chico Lucas stated the vetoes uphold constitutional integrity and emphasize targeting organized crime leaders, including 'drug lords' and financiers, with implementation measures to be developed within 180 days.

item.title: PM morta em SP: Tarcísio fala pela 1ª vez sobre o caso e defende prisão do tenente-coronel
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) spoke for the first time about the killing of soldier Gisele Alves Santana by her husband, Lieutenant Colonel Geraldo Leite Rosa Neto. He defended the arrest of the military officer and condemned the crime, emphasizing the need for strict justice against domestic violence, particularly within the security forces.

item.title: Flávio questiona prisão domiciliar temporária de Bolsonaro: “Decisão exótica”
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) called the STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes' decision to grant 90 days of humanitarian house arrest to former President Jair Bolsonaro for broncopneumonia recovery 'exotic'. While acknowledging it as a first step toward justice, Flávio criticized the measure as inadequate. The remarks were made in an interview with GloboNews.

item.title: Malafaia poupa Moraes ao comentar prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Pastor Silas Malafaia, known for frequent criticism of Justice Alexandre de Moraes, adopted a conciliatory tone when commenting on the decision to allow Jair Bolsonaro to serve his sentence under house arrest. Rather than attacking the magistrate, Malafaia limited his remarks to the benefit granted to the former president, refraining from extending criticism to the Supreme Court.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula participa de 3ª Conferência Nacional de Desenvolvimento Rural
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva attends the 3rd National Conference on Sustainable and Solidary Rural Development in Brasília, alongside Minister Paulo Teixeira and other officials. The event, held from March 24–27, aims to develop policy proposals for rural, water, and forest populations, following over 40,000 participants in preparatory stages and the generation of around 1,000 public policy proposals.

item.title: Lula sanciona o PL Antifacção com vetos sobre a divisão de recursos e equiparação de penas
item.description: President Lula signed the Anti-Faction Bill but vetoed provisions that would have equalized sentences between members and non-members of criminal factions and directed seized assets to state and federal district funds, citing risks of criminalizing social movements and reducing federal revenue. The core of the bill, which toughens penalties and creates new crimes against criminal organizations, remains intact. The vetoes may be overridden by Congress in a joint session.

item.title: Com a saída de Ratinho, se Zema caminhar ao centro poderá surpreender
item.description: With the potential withdrawal of Ratinho Junior from the presidential race, Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (NOVO) could surprise by positioning himself in the ideological center, appealing to the 'neither-nor' electorate. Although Felipe Nunes of Quaest describes a 'calcified' electorate favoring Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, about one-third of voters remain uncommitted, opening space for a moderate alternative.

item.title: Lula escala Messias para se aproximar de Mendonça e ajudar a construir pontes no STF
item.description: President Lula plans to nominate Attorney General Jorge Messias to the STF to ease tensions in the Court, particularly with Justice André Mendonça, with whom Messias shares evangelical affiliation and a history of conflict with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. The move comes amid a dispute over the extension of the INSS CPI, linked to the Banco Master scandal, which will be voted on by the STF plenary next Thursday. Lula aims to use Messias’ nomination as a bridge to calm the polarized atmosphere in the Supreme Court, after delaying the nomination due to political resistance.

item.title: Justiça decide se Flávio Bolsonaro terá de pagar R$ 15 mil por uso indevido de foto com crianças
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Justice will decide this Wednesday (25) whether to uphold the ruling that ordered Senator Flávio Bolsonaro to pay R$ 15,000 in moral damages to photographer Ana Carolina Fernandes for the unauthorized use of a 2018 photo of two children. Bolsonaro shared the image in 2020 with a caption claiming the girls were 'militants against faith' and had their minds 'seized by cultural Marxism,' distorting the original anti-Jair Bolsonaro protest context. Although he deleted the post after backlash, the first-instance court already ruled he violated copyright and harmed the photographer’s honor.

item.title: Após conversa com Caiado, Kassab se reunirá com Leite na 4ª feira
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, said he will meet with Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite on Wednesday (March 25, 2026) to discuss a potential presidential candidacy for Leite in 2026. The meeting comes a day after Kassab spoke with Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado, also mentioned as a possible PSD presidential candidate. The activity follows the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior as a pre-candidate due to family reasons.

item.title: MDB é o partido da democracia e tem projeto, diz Baleia Rossi
item.description: MDB president Baleia Rossi stated the party is 'the party of democracy' and has a project for Brazil during a solemn session celebrating its 60th anniversary. He praised historical figures like Tancredo Neves, José Sarney, Ulysses Guimarães, and Michel Temer for their courage in building Brazilian democracy. Founded in 1966 during the dictatorship, the MDB was the only permitted opposition party and played a central role in redemocratization, though it will not field its own presidential candidate in 2026.

item.title: PT quer atacar imagem de um Flávio “moderado”
item.description: The PT's executive committee has decided to attack the image of moderation surrounding Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president in 2026, aiming to dismantle the perception that he is distinct from his father, Jair Bolsonaro. The strategy was defined in a meeting on Tuesday (24) and targets voters who see Flávio as a more moderate alternative to Bolsonarism.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei antifacção sem vetos polêmicos, como proibição de voto e de auxílio-reclusão
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the anti-gang crime law without vetoing controversial provisions, including the ban on voting and prison assistance for pretrial detainees. The measure aims to strengthen the fight against organized crime, retaining disputed articles that sparked debate in Congress.

item.title: Cassado pela Câmara, Deltan Dallagnol anuncia pré-candidatura ao Senado no Paraná
item.description: Deltan Dallagnol (Novo) announced his pre-candidacy for the Senate in Paraná, following the annulment of his 2023 mandate by the TSE, though he remains eligible. His candidacy is part of a PL-led 'Lava Jato' slate, which includes federal deputy Felipe Barros as Senate candidate and former judge Sergio Moro as pre-candidate for governor, backed by PL president Valdemar Costa Neto and attended by Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que endurece combate a facções criminosas com dois vetos
item.description: President Lula signed the Anti-Faction law, which toughens penalties against organized crime by defining criminal factions as groups of three or more using violence or intimidation to control territories, increasing sentences to up to 40 years in prison. Lula vetoed two provisions: one allowing individuals to be charged without proof of faction ties (due to concerns over criminalizing social movements), and another directing seized assets to state and federal district funds, arguing it would undermine the National Public Security Fund.

item.title: Tarcísio celebra prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro e defende “tratamento humano”
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas praised Justice Alexandre de Moraes' decision to grant Jair Bolsonaro 90 days of humanitarian house arrest due to bronchopneumonia, stating it is fair to ensure the former president receives proper medical treatment.

item.title: Ministro diz que vai apresentar a Lula decreto sobre combate ao crime
item.description: The Minister of Justice and Public Security, Wellington César, stated he will present to President Lula a proposed decree aimed at combating crime. The initiative seeks to strengthen national public security actions.

item.title: Lula sanciona PL antifacção e diz que Brasil quer prender os "magnatas do crime"
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the Anti-Factions Law, approved by Congress based on a government bill. In his speech, Lula stated the law gives Brazil the chance to arrest "crime magnates," not just small-time offenders from the periphery, and mentioned he spoke with Donald Trump about Brazil's willingness to contribute its expertise in fighting organized crime, drug, and arms trafficking. The law was signed with vetoes, though the specific articles vetoed have not yet been disclosed by the Planalto Palace.

item.title: As intenções de voto de Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro no Ceará, segundo novo Datafolha
item.description: Lula leads with 54% voting intention in Ceará for the 2026 elections, followed by Flávio Bolsonaro with 27%. Lula holds strong support among Catholics (64%) and most segments, while Flávio leads among evangelicals (42% to 34%).

item.title: Lula alterna entre discurso punitivista e garantista em sanção do projeto de lei antifacção
item.description: President Lula sanctioned the bill to combat criminal factions, alternating between punitive and guarantist rhetoric. He praised the measure as vital for public safety while emphasizing the need to uphold fundamental rights and prevent abuses. The sanction reflects an effort to balance hardline crime-fighting with constitutional safeguards.

item.title: Caiado “presidente seria um privilégio para o Brasil”, diz Kassab
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, stated that it would be a great privilege for Brazil to have Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás, as president of the Republic. The remark came one day after Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior announced he would not seek re-election in 2026, fueling speculation about potential PSD candidacies in the presidential race.

item.title: PSD não discute alianças para 2026 neste momento, diz Kassab
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab stated the party is not discussing alliances for the 2026 elections at this time, focusing instead on selecting its own presidential candidate, with governors Ronaldo Caiado (GO) and Eduardo Leite (RS) under consideration following the withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior (PR). He criticized party coalitions as a 'jabuticaba' of Brazilian politics and highlighted Silvia Abravanel’s affiliation as a strengthening move. The PSD aims to finalize its presidential candidate by the end of the month, before the gubernatorial incompatibilization deadline.

item.title: 'Melhorou para Bolsonaro', diz Malafaia sobre decisão de Moraes de prisão domiciliar
item.description: Pastor Silas Malafaia commented in a mild tone on the decision by STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes to grant house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro, stating that the situation 'improved for Bolsonaro'. Malafaia, a longtime ally of the former president, acknowledged the benefit of the measure despite his history of public conflicts with the minister.

item.title: Papo Antagonista: Bolsonaro em casa e Caiado no aquecimento
item.description: On Papo Antagonista, the discussion covered Jair Bolsonaro's house arrest, the likely presidential candidacy of Ronaldo Caiado for the PSD, and the controversial Nike campaign involving the Brazilian national team.

item.title: Governo Lula bloqueia só R$ 1,6 bi em gastos em ano eleitoral
item.description: The Lula government froze R$ 1.6 billion in discretionary spending in 2026 to meet the primary surplus target of 0.25% of GDP, avoiding a R$ 59.8 billion deficit. The decision followed a review of the budget approved in December 2025 and enacted in January 2026, with the TCU allowing flexibility to target the center of the fiscal goal. The freeze applied only to one expenditure category, with no contingency needed, aiming for a R$ 3.5 billion surplus.

item.title: As alfinetadas de líder da oposição em Moraes mesmo após domiciliar concedida a Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Rogério Marinho, leader of the opposition in the Senate, criticized Minister Alexandre de Moraes' decision to grant house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro, claiming unequal treatment and the instrumentalization of justice for political persecution. He compared the case to Fernando Collor, who had no time limits, and to Lula, convicted of corruption, arguing Bolsonaro is being targeted by a constructed narrative to remove him from public life. Marinho urged judicial restraint and called for electing a 2026 Congress with the courage to restore separation of powers and check Supreme Court overreach.

item.title: Lula sanciona PL Antifacção com vetos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) signed into law the Anti-Gang Bill, establishing the Legal Framework for Combating Organized Crime, with vetoes on provisions deemed unconstitutional. The law creates new criminal offenses targeting criminal factions, with penalties of up to 40 years in prison, aiming to strengthen state action against organized crime. The signing took place on Tuesday, the 24th, following congressional approval.

item.title: Motta afirma que Lei Antifacção é uma grande demonstração de maturidade política
item.description: Chamber President Hugo Motta stated that the Anti-Faction Law, signed by President Lula, is a major demonstration of political maturity among the Branches of Government. He emphasized that the law establishes a new legal framework against organized crime, including offenses such as structured social domain (20–40 years in prison) and facilitation of such domain (12–20 years), along with restrictions like bans on amnesty and conditional release. The law was named the Raul Jungmann Law in honor of the late former Justice Minister.

item.title: Sheinbaum estudia una alianza entre Pemex y Petrobras para explorar las aguas profundas del Golfo de México
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum confirmed she received a proposal from Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva for a potential partnership between Pemex and Petrobras to explore deep waters in the Gulf of Mexico. She acknowledged the idea was raised in a phone call but stressed no decision has been made. Lula also invited her to visit Brazil in June.

item.title: Lula sanciona PL Antifacção com veto e sem Alcolumbre no Planalto
item.description: Lula signed the Anti-Criminal Organization Bill with vetoes on provisions deemed legally risky, such as vague expansion of criminal offenses and broad prohibition of prison assistance, while preserving the bill's core toughening measures. The signing ceremony took place without Senate President Davi Alcolumbre and was attended by Chamber President Hugo Motta. The law creates two heinous crimes targeting criminal factions and establishes asset forfeiture distribution between federal and state governments based on investigative authority.

item.title: Conselho do PSD escolheu Caiado para disputar Presidência, diz Bornhausen
item.description: The PSD council selected Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate after Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior withdrew from the race, citing succession risks and rising support for Sergio Moro in the state. Caiado was chosen in a meeting on Monday, March 23, 2026, holding a slight edge over Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite in the Quaest poll of March 11, 2026 (4% to 3%, with a 2-point margin of error). Leite indicated he may run for the Senate instead.

item.title: Governo Lula congela R$ 1,6 bi em despesas do Orçamento de 2026
item.description: The Lula government announced a freeze of R$ 1.6 billion in the 2026 budget expenditures to offset increased projected spending on the BPC (Continuous Benefit) and education programs. The measure aims to maintain fiscal balance amid rising pressure on public accounts. The freeze affects various discretionary spending areas, though specific impacted sectors were not detailed.

item.title: Caso eleito, Flávio promete Ministério da Segurança Pública
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) pledged to create a Ministry of Public Security if elected president in 2026. The statement was made in a video posted on social media, alongside federal deputy Guilherme Derrite (PP-SP), former Secretary of Public Security of São Paulo.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei contra facções criminosas com veto, após pressão e divergências no Congresso
item.description: President Lula signed the anti-organized crime law, known as the 'PL Antifacção', after pressure and disagreements in Congress. He vetoed a provision that could have extended penalties to actions deemed equivalent to criminal organization activities, fearing the criminalization of social movements and protests. The government plans to issue a decree next week to regulate the law, outlining six strategic implementation axes.

item.title: Governo Lula estima superavit de R$ 3,5 bi em 2026
item.description: The Lula government projects a primary surplus of R$3.5 billion in 2026, below the budgeted target of R$43.3 billion, due to a freeze of R$1.6 billion in discretionary spending to meet fiscal rules. The measure was enabled by TCU clearance, avoiding a larger spending block in an election year. The 2025 deficit was declared met after excluding atypical expenses, though it would have been higher without such adjustments.

item.title: Deltan Dallagnol tenta voltar ao Congresso com pré-candidatura ao Senado pelo PL do Paraná
item.description: Former federal deputy Deltan Dallagnol (Novo) announced his pre-candidacy for the Senate in Paraná, after having his candidacy registration annulled by the TSE in 2023 for serving as a federal prosecutor while facing internal disciplinary proceedings related to Operation Lava Jato. He claims to be fully eligible and aims to form a 'Lava Jato' ticket with Senator Sergio Moro (PL), who recently joined the party and will run for governor of Paraná. The event also included Moro’s wife, federal deputy Rosângela Moro, and was attended by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president.

item.title: Deltan Dallagnol tenta voltar ao Congresso com pré-candidatura ao Senado no Paraná
item.description: Deltan Dallagnol (Novo) announced his pre-candidacy for the Senate in Paraná, after having his candidacy registration annulled by the TSE in 2023, but without being declared ineligible. He is part of a 'Lava Jato' ticket with Senator Sergio Moro (PL) and Filipe Barros (PL), aiming for the state government. The event featured support from Flávio Bolsonaro and the party affiliation of Rosângela Moro, reinforcing alliances between former Lava Jato figures and Bolsonarists.

item.title: Ao vivo: Com Motta, Lula sanciona o PL Antifacção no Planalto
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed the Anti-Faction Bill (PL 5.582/2025) at the Planalto Palace, with the participation of Chamber President Hugo Motta. The bill establishes a legal framework to combat organized crime, though certain provisions may be challenged in the Supreme Court for alleged unconstitutionality. The government's strategy was to sanction the Congress-approved text and rely on judicial review to address problematic sections.

item.title: Flávio critica domiciliar temporária a Bolsonaro: 'Nenhuma lógica'
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) criticized Justice Alexandre de Moraes' decision to grant temporary house arrest to former President Jair Bolsonaro, calling it 'exotic' and lacking legal basis, stating it does not exist in Brazilian law. He questioned the logic of allowing Bolsonaro to return to the 19th Military Police Battalion in DF if his health improves, given that the location is considered a risk for worsening his condition. The measure, initially valid for 90 days, was justified by Moraes based on Bolsonaro's recovery from bronchopneumonia.

item.title: Em busca de viés antissistema, Flávio Bolsonaro se alia ao lavajatismo contra o PT
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro is positioning himself as an anti-system candidate by aligning with former Lava Jato figures Sergio Moro and Deltan Dallagnol to defeat the PT and capture voters dissatisfied with Lula but reluctant to support him due to his Bolsonaro ties. The strategy leverages the anti-corruption image of his allies to counter scandals like Banco Master and attract 'third way' voters seeking an outsider, according to Quaest research.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro “aceita” domiciliar, mas critica prazo de 90 dias
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro welcomed the humanitarian house arrest granted by Minister Alexandre de Moraes of the STF as a correct decision by the Court. However, they criticized the 90-day fixed term for the measure, calling it excessive and requesting an earlier review.

item.title: 'É uma decisão exótica', diz Flávio após Moraes autorizar prisão domiciliar para Bolsonaro por 90 dias
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro called the decision by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to grant temporary house arrest to former President Jair Bolsonaro for 90 days 'exotic' and 'contradictory.' He criticized the lack of legal basis for the measure and questioned the logic of returning his father to prison if his health improves at home. He also stated that the family, particularly Michelle Bolsonaro, must provide constant supervision, possibly with medical or nursing support.
